channel.title: flove making-of — workflow questions
channel.description: New workflow design questions from flove interviews.

item.title: What is the release cadence for flove?
item.description:
• Weekly — Release every week. Fast iteration, potentially rushed. • Monthly — Release every month. Balanced pace, predictable. • Quarterly — Release every quarter. Slower, more polished. • When ready — No fixed cadence — ship when the release is good. Flexible, unpredictable.
★ decided: — · ◇ recommended: When ready

item.title: How should flove handle breaking changes?
item.description:
• Deprecation warnings — Warn users/developers, give time to migrate. Gentle, requires tooling. • Automatic migration — Script migrates old apps to new standard. Convenient, risky if wrong. • Manual updates — Each app updates itself. Simple, potentially slow.
★ decided: — · ◇ recommended: Deprecation warnings

item.title: Should flove adopt a formal RFC process?
item.description:
• Yes, formal RFC — Structured proposal process — draft, review, decide. More process, clearer decisions. • No, keep informal — Decisions happen in specs and conversations. Flexible, less structured. • Lightweight RFC — Template-based proposals, no formal voting. Balanced.
★ decided: — · ◇ recommended: No, keep informal

item.title: Should flove have automated testing for apps?
item.description:
• Yes, full test suite — Unit + integration + visual regression. Catches bugs, adds complexity. • Light tests only — Just HTML validation + basic smoke tests. Catches obvious issues. • No tests — Keep manual verification. Simplest, relies on Marc's attention.
★ decided: — · ◇ recommended: Light tests only

item.title: How should flove handle app dependencies?
item.description:
• Documented + versioned — Each dependency pinned to a version, documented. Reproducible builds. • Just documented — List dependencies but don't pin versions. Informal but useful. • No documentation — Keep dependencies implicit. Simplest, least reproducible.
★ decided: — · ◇ recommended: Just documented

item.title: Should flove apps have performance budgets?
item.description:
• Yes, strict budgets — E.g., <50KB HTML, <10KB JS, <20KB CSS. Keeps apps lean, adds constraints. • Soft guidelines — Suggested limits, not enforced. Informal, less pressure. • No budgets — No size limits — each app is as big as it needs to be. Free-form.
★ decided: — · ◇ recommended: Soft guidelines

item.title: Should flove adopt a monorepo or multi-repo strategy?
item.description:
• Monorepo — Everything in one repo. Simple, atomic commits, but grows large. • Multi-repo — Separate repos per project. Cleaner boundaries, harder cross-project changes. • Hybrid — Core in monorepo, major projects in separate repos. Balanced.
★ decided: — · ◇ recommended: Monorepo

item.title: Should flove have a formal deprecation policy?
item.description:
• Yes, formal policy — E.g., 6 months notice, migration guide, version marker. Professional, more process. • Informal notice — Announce deprecation in changelog, no fixed timeline. Flexible. • No policy — Just remove/replace when ready. Simplest, potentially abrupt.
★ decided: — · ◇ recommended: Informal notice
